tweets.text:
SpaceX has developed a prototype for a handset-like device designed to reshape how humans interact with artificial intelligence: WSJ

The prototype features a sleek design that is slimmer than an iPhone; it was shown to some investors and other stakeholders ahead of the company’s IPO

The prototype was designed to run on a proprietary operating system and integrate AI technology from SpaceX’s xAI. The device would use a Qualcomm Snapdragon chipset

tweets.text:
BREAKING: Total US production of crude oil and petroleum products rose by +254,000 barrels per day in April, to a record 21.84 million barrels per day.

YoY, total production surged +1.01 million barrels per day, or +4.9%.

Crude oil production alone increased by +216,000 barrels per day in April, to a record 13.93 million barrels per day.

Since the 2008 Financial Crisis, total US petroleum output has QUADRUPLED.

Over the same period, crude oil production has soared +250%.

US oil production is booming.

tweets.text:
BREAKING: Commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has surged to 10 million barrels of crude oil per day, per Bloomberg.

Details include:

1. Traffic has increased sharply since the 60-day "Memorandum of Understanding" was signed

2. Oil volumes through the Strait of Hormuz still stand ~50% below pre-war levels

3. Uncertainty still exists around what will happen with the Strait of Hormuz once the MoU expires

US oil prices are down over -40% from the recent high.

tweets.text:
Everyone knows that July is seasonally bullish.

But, over the past five years, it is the most negative month for the momentum factor (top 10%/bottom 10%).

Heading into July, momentum has been on a historic tear...makes sense to see the broadening/rotation (excerpt h2 outlook). https://t.co/XIstKBSC2s

tweets.text:
🚢Hormuz Vessel Crossing Update: Recovery Stalls at 19 — Tankers 10, Dry Cargo 9

Commercial vessel crossings through the Strait of Hormuz have slipped to 19 over the latest 24-hour rolling window — 10 tankers and 9 dry cargo ships — down from 24 yesterday and a full 67% below the June 24 recovery peak of 57. What looked like a rebuilding of traffic into late June has flattened into four straight readings stuck in the high-teens-to-20s, well short of anything resembling normal.

Breaking it out by vessel type tells the story of the stall. Both segments spiked on June 24 — 24 tankers and 33 dry cargo ships, each the strongest single-day count since the late-February collapse. But the dry-bulk dash has unwound fastest, off 73% from that peak versus 58% for tankers. The result: tankers have quietly reclaimed the slim majority of the flow (roughly 53%), closer to their pre-conflict share, but on a fraction of the volume.

Powered by our commercial vessel crossing index, using AIS data refreshed every 30 minutes.

Breaking it out by vessel type tells the story of the stall. Both segments spiked on June 24 — 24 tankers and 33 dry cargo ships, each the strongest single-day count since the late-February collapse. But the dry-bulk dash has unwound fastest, off 73% from that peak versus 58% for tankers. The result: tankers have quietly reclaimed the slim majority of the flow (roughly 53%), closer to their pre-conflict share, but on a fraction of the volume.

tweets.text:
Citi: "Against this backdrop we update our forecasts, in the base case assuming no new [Bitcoin ETF] inflows in the next 12m. This reduces our central BTC forecast to $82k and for ETH to $2.2k. In our prior publications and research we have emphasized the outsized effects of ETF flows in explaining (the variation of) returns - Digital Asset Take: What’s Driving Bitcoin? (It’s Flows). 
The lack of legislative progress and negative sentiment in the sector has seen a reversal of flows YTD. Our targets were predicated on increased investor and financial advisor interest leading to gradual adoption and inflows from these traditional-financial sources. We think this process is likely put on pause until we see a catalyst. The average ETF holder is now underwater and prices are below their pre-2024 US election levels.
Legislation is one potential catalyst but seems unlikely before November’s US midterms. The return of “de-basement fears” could also increase investor interest but Warsh’s hawkish debut reduces those risks in the near-term in our view. Consequently, we reduce our flow assumption in the next 12m to zero in the base case and negative in the bear case. It is important to note that crypto markets are volatile, and sentiment driven which makes them reflexive in terms of price action, on-chain/off-chain activity, and flows. This increases forecast uncertainty but can cause abrupt changes in sentiment."
tweets.full_text:
Citi: "Against this backdrop we update our forecasts, in the base case assuming no new [Bitcoin ETF] inflows in the next 12m. This reduces our central BTC forecast to $82k and for ETH to $2.2k. In our prior publications and research we have emphasized the outsized effects of ETF flows in explaining (the variation of) returns - Digital Asset Take: What’s Driving Bitcoin? (It’s Flows). 
The lack of legislative progress and negative sentiment in the sector has seen a reversal of flows YTD. Our targets were predicated on increased investor and financial advisor interest leading to gradual adoption and inflows from these traditional-financial sources. We think this process is likely put on pause until we see a catalyst. The average ETF holder is now underwater and prices are below their pre-2024 US election levels.
Legislation is one potential catalyst but seems unlikely before November’s US midterms. The return of “de-basement fears” could also increase investor interest but Warsh’s hawkish debut reduces those risks in the near-term in our view. Consequently, we reduce our flow assumption in the next 12m to zero in the base case and negative in the bear case. It is important to note that crypto markets are volatile, and sentiment driven which makes them reflexive in terms of price action, on-chain/off-chain activity, and flows. This increases forecast uncertainty but can cause abrupt changes in sentiment."

tweets.text:
"And then along came blockchains."

Turing Award winner Barbara Liskov on how PBFT — practical Byzantine fault tolerance, now foundational to modern consensus — started as a PhD student answering a DARPA RFP:

"I had a student, Miguel Castro, who was looking for a PhD thesis, and I suggested to Miguel that he look at the RFPs that DARPA had put out … He found an RFP that was looking for ways to handle the malicious attacks that were going on on the internet."

"When Miguel and I finished the work on practical Byzantine fault tolerance, we thought that at some point people would start to use this. … It was a delay of about 10 years before people started to use it."
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
@Tim_Roughgarden @ittaia First Principles Ep. 2 w/ Barbara Liskov

Every blockchain alive today leans on replication ideas worked out in the 1980s, by a Turing Award winner who wasn’t thinking about how it might apply to money at all.

Hosted by @Tim_Roughgarden with @ittaia.

00:00 How do systems stay https://t.co/mR8h4TLHgF
tweets.full_text:
"And then along came blockchains."

Turing Award winner Barbara Liskov on how PBFT — practical Byzantine fault tolerance, now foundational to modern consensus — started as a PhD student answering a DARPA RFP:

"I had a student, Miguel Castro, who was looking for a PhD thesis, and I suggested to Miguel that he look at the RFPs that DARPA had put out … He found an RFP that was looking for ways to handle the malicious attacks that were going on on the internet."

"When Miguel and I finished the work on practical Byzantine fault tolerance, we thought that at some point people would start to use this. … It was a delay of about 10 years before people started to use it."

tweets.text:
Semiconductor stocks were up 237% over the last 14 months, surpassing the 234% surge during the peak of the dot-com bubble.

The AI mania has taken semis to a place only seen once before.

History doesn’t repeat, but extremes tend to rhyme.

Video: https://t.co/LBXJJFkuG1

tweets.text:
I stated that I ignore Bitcoin as an investment in the 1st half of midterm years.

The second half of midterm years usually marks the accumulation zone/market cycle bottom.

Today (July 1st), Bitcoin put in a new low, justifying the views I held for the 1st half of the year.

tweets.text: Good Morning from Germany, where the fertility rate has fallen to 1.32 children per woman, the lowest since 1996. Only 654,241 babies were born in 2025, the fewest in postwar history. Even Lower Saxony, Germany’s most fertile state, slipped below 1.4. https://t.co/v50PqPSm49

tweets.text:
June 30, 2026, SPX is trading near the upper end of its 6-month range while the Total Put/Call Ratio sits at 0.88.

Protection demand cooled fast from the June spike. This is not a panic reading.

For traders waiting on a fear-driven entry, the next real signal needs a sharper spike in put demand.

Spot when options fear reaches actionable extremes with the Total Put/Call Ratio: https://t.co/xxRX5UzgB1

tweets.text: People who argue that compute will remain scarce indefinitely should look up “Turing machine”. Computation is completely universal: It can be done in a vacuum tube, transistor, or even by a polarized photon within a quantum computer. https://t.co/XmbasvnWmx
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: It appears compute is not all that scarce if one of the biggest AI names is starting to sell it
